# Aberdeen Group Archive — Kastner Collection

> A 933-study, 19,175-observation Frictionless Data Package archive of Peter S. Kastner's 50+ year career in technology industry analysis (Prime Computer, Stratus, DEC, Aberdeen Group, Yankee Group, Obian Group, Adoptex). Each study is a self-contained dataset with `studies.csv`, `entities.csv`, `technologies.csv`, `observations.csv`, and a `datapackage.json` Frictionless descriptor. Built for both human research and machine analysis.

The archive is structured for retrieval: every study has provenance, a structured abstract, and ratings for **importance** (historical significance), **relevance** (ongoing applicability), and **prescience** (predictive accuracy as judged from 2025).

- [Repo README](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/README.md) — full archive overview, key reading paths, employer breakdown
- [_master_studies.csv](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/_master_studies.csv) — 933-row master index of every study
- [_master_entities.csv](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/_master_entities.csv) — deduplicated entity dictionary across the corpus
- [_master_technologies.csv](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/_master_technologies.csv) — deduplicated technology dictionary
- [_MASTER_INDEX.md](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/_MASTER_INDEX.md) — human-readable index by collection

## Highlighted Studies

Curator-selected entry points to the archive. Each is a complete Frictionless Data Package.

- [Prime Computer — Industry Product Requirements Plan (Five-Year Plan, July 1981, COMPANY CO](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author/employer/prime-computer/prime-industry-product-5yr-plan-1981-0a3546) — 1981-07-15 — *Peter Kastner & Others, Prime Computer Market Planning* — Historically very early five-year industry product plan (July 1981, COMPANY CONFIDENTIAL). Pre-PC era enterprise minicomputer strategy: 32-bit superminis, CAD/CAM, office automation, and the early case for industry-vertical product positioning.
- [A Fault-Tolerant Transaction Processing Environment (Stratus/32, IEEE Database Engineering](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author/employer/stratus-computer/ieee-db-stratus32-1983-1d4564) — 1983-06-01 — *Peter S. Kastner — Stratus Computer* — Stratus/32 fault-tolerant transaction processing — IEEE Database Engineering Bulletin, June 1983. Anticipates two-phase commit, location-transparent file systems, RAID-1 mirroring, hot-swap modules, and call-home telemetry years before they became industry standard.
- [Report of Peter S. Kastner: Intelligent Electronics Inc. v. Andersen Consulting LLP](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author/kastner-ie-v-andersen-expert-report-3de98a) — 1999-06-21 — *Peter S. Kastner* — Expert witness report (June 1999) for Intelligent Electronics Inc. v. Andersen Consulting LLP. Full economic and technical opinion on PC distribution channel collapse, AS/400 longevity, and the consolidation of two-tier distribution. Predictions on IE non-viability and AS/400 staying power both validated.
- [Safeway Damages Report: Expert Opinion on Consonus Data Center Incident](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author/safeway-damages-report-draft-b502f4) — 2003-12-01 — *Hugh Bishop with Peter Kastner* — December 2003 expert damages opinion analyzing the February 2002 fire-suppression-system discharge at Consonus data center and its impact on Safeway Inc.'s 40-node Teradata data warehouse. Hardware valuation, business interruption costing, and the strategic value of mission-critical analytics infrastructure.
- [Project Examples / Maxtor Storage (RAMP)](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/Project%20Examples/Maxtor%20Storage) — *Aberdeen Group, 2003-2004* — Complete project file set from the Maxtor Midline Storage market-creation engagement led by Stephen DiFranco (CMO, Maxtor) and Peter Kastner. Includes RAMP (Rapid Accurate Market Prediction) survey results, interview notes (Applied Materials, Inovant, Kaiser Permanente, State Street, Timex), white papers, engagement letters, and the Flash demonstration tool. A worked example of the Aberdeen RAMP methodology applied to creating a new storage category. *Cautionary case study: creating a market category is expensive and high-risk.*
- [Project Examples / Sun AS-400 (RAMP)](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/Project%20Examples/Sun%20AS-400) — *Aberdeen Group, 2002* — Sun Microsystems-commissioned RAMP study targeting AS/400 (iSeries) replacement opportunities. Five-package set: main report, market data tables, executive presentation, top iSeries ISV analysis, and worldwide IT spending forecast 2002-2005. Demonstrates how a vendor uses external analyst research to position against an entrenched competitor.
- [Asian Virus Casts Shadow Over Supply Chain: SARS Impact on Asia/Pacific Semiconductor and ](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author/2003-sars-electronics-supply-chain-ec0375) — 2003-04-16 — *Russ Craig with Peter S. Kastner* — April 2003 supply-chain risk assessment — *Asian Virus Casts Shadow Over Supply Chain*. Explicitly identified the 'no-second-source PRC component' risk (AC/DC power supplies, low-level assemblies) **17 years before COVID-19** validated the same thesis with PPE, chip, and consumer-electronics shortages. A foundational early-2000s supply-chain prescience document.

## Fifteen High-Prescience Calls

Studies in which Kastner's published predictions have been validated by subsequent industry events. Filtered from the 175 Kastner-authored studies rated `prescience: high` in `_master_studies.csv`, ranked by importance and rationale strength, and curated to span 1981-2010 across fault tolerance, mainframe economics, government IT procurement, RDBMS, operating systems, channel economics, IT spending, and computing-architecture continuum strategy. Two studies that previously appeared on this list — IE v. Andersen and the SARS supply-chain insight — receive their full curator treatment in **Highlighted Studies** above and are referenced there rather than duplicated here.

 1. [The Fault Tolerant Revolution — Stratus/32 launch advertisement](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author/stratus-fault-tolerant-revolution-ad-198-797e0e) — 1981-07-01 — *Stratus Computer, Inc. (marketing; Peter S. Kastner era)* — Ad predicted 'hardware costs will continue to fall' while 'software [people] costs will rise' — subsequently validated as Moore's Law and salary inflation tracked exactly as claimed for 40+ years.
 2. [Stratus Computer Corporate Overview, November 1982](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author/stratus-corporate-overview-1982-11-a7e367) — 1982-11-01 — *Stratus Computer, Inc. (corporate marketing — Kastner-era ar* — Predicted that 'businesses and institutions of all sizes will put the key functions of their operations on-line via a computer system or network' — pre-Internet, this anticipated the universal connectivity of modern commerce. Forecasted 20-.
 3. [IBM AS/400 vs. HP 3000 Marketplace Analysis for Stanley-Vidmar](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author/1989-vidmar-as400-hp3000-and-metadata-a32d04) — 1989-01-01 — *Peter S. Kastner / Aberdeen Group* — Aberdeen correctly predicted the AS/400's performance shortcomings and maturity issues in 1989; HP 3000 indeed outlasted AS/400's original product line, running until 2010; Stanley-Vidmar remained part of The Stanley Works and is now Vidmar.
 4. [Unisys vs. Florida HRS — Expert Witness Memoir (Tallahassee, c.1990-1991)](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author/memoirs/unisys-florida-hrs-expert-witness-tallahassee-5b4524) — 2026-01-12 *(memoir of c.1990-1991 testimony)* — *Peter S. Kastner* — Testified in the Florida Senate Chamber that the IBM/EDS DB2-on-mainframe architecture could not meet response-time requirements for distributed HRS workloads. Unisys lost the bid; the IBM/EDS implementation then failed catastrophically (a $200M fiasco), helping motivate the **Clinger-Cohen Act of 1996** governing federal IT procurement.
 5. [Ten Issues for IBM Mainframe MIS Executives](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author/10ibmiss-f66945) — 1991-01-01 — *Peter S. Kastner* — Kastner's prediction that IBM mainframe dominance would erode under cost and architectural pressures proved accurate; the 1990s saw a massive migration to distributed and open systems, and IBM was eventually forced to dramatically reinvent.
 6. [Front-Ends and Apple Computer in the 1990s](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author/1991-apple-c-s-e9ffd7) — 1991-01-09 — *Peter S. Kastner / Aberdeen Group* — Aberdeen accurately predicted: client-server maturity in the mid-1990s (confirmed); Apple's enterprise market share staying below 15% through the decade; Microsoft Windows as the dominant client platform; IBM PC inertia as the primary barri.
 7. [Open Systems: A Technology Status Report](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author/nti-2-open-systems-1dd3af) — 1993-01-01 — *Peter S. Kastner, John Logan, Thomas Willmott* — Predictions proved accurate: Intel x86 dominance grew as forecast, Windows/DOS dominated the near-term desktop, client-server displaced proprietary midrange, and integration responsibility shifted to customers as described.
 8. [RDBMS Report Card](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author/nti-6-rdbms-technology-48f4aa) — 1993-04-01 — *Peter S. Kastner, John Logan, Thomas Willmott* — Oracle's predicted dominance proved accurate; the forecast that RDBMS would become the primary enterprise application platform was fully borne out by the ERP and data-warehouse booms of the mid-to-late 1990s.
 9. [Can Parallel-Scalable RDBMSs Break the Downsizing Logjam?](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author/informdsa-9f8bcf) — 1995 — *Peter S. Kastner / Aberdeen Group* — Correctly predicted 3-5x growth in information demand by late 1990s; correctly forecast SMP dominance followed by cluster/MPP adoption; parallel-scalable RDBMS as a 'free hardware upgrade' proved prescient.
10. [Welcome to the RDBMS Jungle — Chapters 1-5](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author/jungle-1-5-675c58) — 1995 — *Peter S. Kastner / Aberdeen Group* — Extremely prescient: correctly identified Microsoft SQL Server as future threat; predicted Oracle's unfocused multimedia strategy as weakness; correctly forecast Sybase's architectural stagnation; Informix's replication gap proved fatal in.
11. [Universal Servers: RDBMS Technology for the Next Decade](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author/aberdeen-1995-universal-servers-rdbms-technology-next-decade) — 1995-06-03 — *Peter S. Kastner, Wayne Kernochan (Aberdeen Group)* — Aberdeen's core predictions proved remarkably accurate: RDBMS survived OODBMSs, complex data types did become standard (object-relational became mainstream via PostgreSQL and Oracle), ROLAP became major BI architecture, and LDAP/Internet da.
12. [Microsoft NT Scalability Day: The Emperor Has No Clothes](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author/1997-microsoft-nt-scalability-day--the-e-6460e2) — 1997-05-21 — *Peter S. Kastner* — Kastner's assessment that NT would be 'better in 1998' but was not yet enterprise-ready proved accurate: NT's enterprise limitations persisted through Windows 2000 and beyond. His prediction that NT's trust gap with enterprise customers wou.
13. [Assessing the Damage: Will Fourth Quarter Technology Spending Fall? (Post-9/11 IT Recession Let](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author/trends00-6a553a) — 2001-10-05 — *Peter S. Kastner / Aberdeen Group* — Kastner predicted double-digit Q4 2001 spending decline and 2002 recession; both proved accurate as the post-9/11 economic shock compounded the dot-com bust.
14. [Planning for Emerging Industry-Standard Platforms (Executive White Paper)](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author/planningforemergingindustry-6-40decb) — 2003-08-01 — *Aberdeen Group (Peter S. Kastner)* — Predictions proved accurate: PCI Express became universal (still dominant 2026); DDR2 then DDR3/4/5 displaced DDR; IPMI remained baseline for ~15 years until Redfish succession; server consolidation and industry-standard market-share growth.
15. [Intel's IA Continuum Strategy Anticipates a Much Broader Computing Market](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author/2010-intel-ia-continuum-wp-1-2-fc5653) — 2010 — *Peter S. Kastner* — Correctly identified cloud, the mobile explosion, SSD adoption, and virtualization as dominant 2010s trends; the consumer-to-datacenter architectural-continuum framing proved accurate. One major miss: ARM as a competitive threat to x86 in mobile and later in the datacenter (Apple Silicon, AWS Graviton, NVIDIA Grace).

## Career Arc & Coverage Breadth

For the meta-narrative of the archive — *why* a single career covers this much ground — the archive contains two paired works:

- [Kastner Memoir / *Arguments with Reality*, Volume 1](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/Kastner%20Memoir) — 2026-05-14 — *Peter S. Kastner (memoir, ~32,000 words)* — Full-length first-person narrative: *Arguments with Reality: Fifty Years in Computing, Consulting, and Consequence*. Ingested as 14 chapter-level study packages (Introduction + 10 chapters + Epilogue + About + Appendix) covering 1960–2026: Cape Cod paper-sorting origins, MIT, PHI Computer Services / Chase Manhattan, Arthur D. Little, Prime Computer, Stratus and the fault-tolerant wars, DEC and the TPC, founding Aberdeen Group, the Go-Go Years at scale, expert-witness work, and a synthesis chapter on what fifty years of technology markets teach. 1,242 observations covering every named person, company, technology, dated event, and analytical judgment in the volume.
- [Project Examples / Kastner Technology Breadth Memoir](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/Project%20Examples/kastner-technology-breadth-memoir) — 2026-05-05 — *Peter S. Kastner (memoir, ~4,000 words)* — Quantified breadth across the Kastner-authored + Kastner-quoted corpus (592 of 933 studies): 2,537 distinct technologies / 4,628 mentions / 479 subject domains spanning 1979 to ~2026, organized into 14 thematic rollups (Personal Computers & OS, Databases, Storage & Hardware, Networking & Internet, Mainframes & Midrange, SOA/BPM/Integration, Unix & Open Systems, Semiconductors & Chips, Security & Reliability, ERP & Enterprise Apps, Outsourcing & Services, Displays & Peripherals, AI/Analytics & Emerging, Programming & Dev Tools), with structured `data/observations.csv` tables for raw category distribution, theme rollup, decade distribution, and vendor leaderboard.

## How the Archive is Organized

- [kastner-author/](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author) — 205 top-level studies authored or co-authored by Peter S. Kastner, plus employer/ and dct/ subcollections (1981-2024)
- [kastner-author/employer/](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author/employer) — Studies grouped by employer: Prime Computer, Stratus Computer, DEC, Aberdeen Group, Arthur D. Little, PHI Computer Services, Obian Group
- [kastner-author/dct/](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/kastner-author/dct) — 76 Digital Consumer Technology studies (2002-2003 PC retail price tracking, weekly commentary, RAMP surveys)
- [other-authors/](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/other-authors) — 487 Aberdeen Group studies and adjacent industry analyses by other authors
- [Project Examples/](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/Project%20Examples) — Selected client-engagement project archives (Maxtor, Sun AS-400, and others) with the full RAMP methodology in action
- [Kastner Memoir/](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/Kastner%20Memoir) — Volume 1 of *Arguments with Reality* (2026), split into 14 chapter packages covering 1960–2026 (1,242 observations)
- [_audits/](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/_audits) — Migration logs, codes audits, and quality-control reports
- [_skills/](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/_skills) — The bundled `archival-ingest` skill and assembler used to build and maintain the archive

## Working with the Data

Each study directory contains a Frictionless Data Package: load `datapackage.json` with the `frictionless` Python library, or read the four CSVs in `data/` directly. Every study includes a `scripts/demo_analysis.py` that demonstrates basic queries. Cross-study analysis is supported via `_master_studies.csv`, `_master_entities.csv`, and `_master_technologies.csv` at the repo root.

## Optional

- [llms-full.txt](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/llms-full.txt) — Extended version of this file with full abstracts and prescience rationales for every highlighted study
- [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/CHANGELOG.md) — Repository changelog
- [CITATION.cff](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/CITATION.cff) — Citation metadata for academic use
- [LICENSE](https://github.com/shorttack/aberdeen-group-archive/tree/main/LICENSE) — CC BY 4.0
